objective: Revise the governance Society of Minds source of truth so Codex orchestrates, Opus plans, GPT-5.4 high reviews plans with Spark as fallback/specialist critique, Sonnet implements, Codex QA reviews, Qwen handles bounded local work, Gemma is A/B shadow-only, and Windows is off the active fallback ladder.
scope_boundary: Update STANDARDS.md, local model inventory, docs, runbooks, templates, guardrails, tests, and closeout evidence.
Preserve downstream repo propagation as issue-backed follow-up work after this PR lands.
Do not edit downstream product repositories in this slice.
out_of_scope: Changing production app model selection outside governance policy surfaces.
research_summary: Operator preplanning established the corrected waterfall. Three read-only research agents inspected governance SSOT files, CI/test guardrails, Stage 6 closeout requirements, and downstream propagation surfaces. Findings converged on STANDARDS.md, LAM runtime inventory/tests, Windows routing tests, PR templates, runbooks, and registry mirrors as the required governance SSOT scope.

sprints.name: Governance SSOT routing update
sprints.goal: Make the charter and documentation reflect the approved waterfall and authority boundaries.
tasks: Update STANDARDS.md with the Codex-orchestrated waterfall.
Update runbooks, PR template, service/feature/data/progress mirrors.
Record structured plan, execution scope, cross-review, validation, and closeout artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Spark is documented only as GPT-5.4 plan-review fallback/specialist critique.
Sonnet 4.6 is documented as the primary substantial Worker.
Gemma is documented as A/B shadow-only.
Windows is documented as deprecated/off active waterfall.

sprints.name: Guardrails and tests
sprints.goal: Make stale routing policy fail in local/CI validation.
tasks: Update LAM config and runtime inventory to expose Qwen worker ladder and Gemma shadow authority.
Update LAM policy check to assert Qwen worker ladder, Gemma shadow-only authority, Sonnet worker policy, and Windows off-ladder policy.
Update Windows decision tests so WINDOWS is not a success path.
Update hook wording away from Spark-primary implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime inventory tests assert Windows is off-ladder and Gemma is non-authoritative.
Windows decision tests fail if decide.sh returns WINDOWS.
Policy check fails if old Spark-primary or Windows-active text returns.

specialist_reviews.focus: File-by-file routing policy and documentation surface inventory.
specialist_reviews.summary: Identified core SSOT, runtime runbooks, Codex surfaces, Windows guardrails, and tests that needed alignment.

specialist_reviews.focus: Tests and CI gates that should fail stale Spark-primary, Windows-active, or Gemma-authoritative policy.
specialist_reviews.summary: Identified Windows decision tests, LAM inventory tests, LAM family policy check, hook wording, and PR template as high-value guardrail updates.

specialist_reviews.focus: Stage 6 closeout and downstream follow-up requirements.
specialist_reviews.summary: Confirmed Stage 6 closeout requirements and residual issue-backed follow-up pattern. Downstream repo propagation must remain separate follow-up work.

alternate_model_review.summary: Standards scope requires cross-review evidence. The committed raw/cross-review artifact records the approved routing plan and implementation guardrails.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement governance SSOT, docs, guardrails, tests, and closeout for issue #432 only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Run focused validation, create downstream follow-up issues, complete Stage 6 closeout, and open PR.

material_deviation_rules: If downstream repo files are required, stop and open follow-up issues instead of editing them in this branch.
If Windows tooling deletion becomes necessary, open a separate removal issue rather than expanding this SSOT slice.
If Gemma authority promotion is desired, stop and open a separate evidence/threshold issue.
If Qwen2.5-Coder full-file rewrite work is requested, route around issue #105 limits.
approved_by: operator approval: gov SSOT then downstream as follow-up issues/PRs
